# TUM IT Organization

## Overview

The **Technical University of Munich (TUM)** provides comprehensive IT services to all its schools, departments, and organizational units through two main providers: the **IT Service Center (ITSZ)** and the **Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ)**. Together, they deliver the central IT infrastructure and services that support research, teaching, and administration across the entire university.

## Organizational Structure

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUM IT Organizational Structure"
flowchart TB
    CIO["CIO"]
    CIO --> IMB["Information Management Board"]
    CIO --> DXO["Digital Transformation Office"]
    CIO --> ITSZ["IT Service Center (ITSZ)"]

    ITSZ --> Schools["School IT Operations"]

    subgraph TUMSchools["TUM Schools & Departments"]
        Schools --> CIT["School of CIT"]
        Schools --> ENG["School of Engineering"]
        Schools --> NAT["School of Natural Sciences"]
        Schools --> OTHER["Other Schools..."]
    end

    subgraph External["Infrastructure Partner"]
        LRZ["Leibniz Supercomputing<br/>Centre (LRZ)"]
    end

    ITSZ <--> |collaboration| LRZ
```

## IT Governance

The TUM IT governance structure ensures strategic alignment and efficient service delivery:

| Body | Role |
|------|------|
| **CIO** | Chief Information Officer, strategic IT leadership |
| **Information Management Board** | Strategic IT decisions and prioritization |
| **Digital Transformation Office (DXO)** | Digital transformation initiatives |
| **IT Service Center (ITSZ)** | Central IT operations and service delivery |

## Central IT Services

The IT Service Center and LRZ provide a wide range of services for all TUM members:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUM Central IT Service Portfolio"
mindmap
    root((TUM IT Services))
        Email & Collaboration
            Exchange
            Mailing Lists
            Shared Mailboxes
        Network & Identity
            MWN Network
            VPN Access
            LDAP / Active Directory
        Server Infrastructure
            Virtual Servers
            Server Housing
            Compute Cloud
        Storage
            Online Storage NAS
            Backup Services
        Client Management
            TUM-PC
            MWN-MAC
            Baramundi
        Web Services
            Managed Webservers
            Domain Management
        Software & Licenses
            Campus Licenses
            Framework Agreements
        Certificates
            User Certificates
            Server Certificates
```

## Key Systems and Tools

### TUMonline

**TUMonline** is TUM's central campus management system and the primary administrative platform:

- **URL**: [campus.tum.de](https://campus.tum.de)
- **Purpose**: User management, group administration, identity management, function objects
- **Access**: Available to all TUM members with role-based permissions

### TUM Wiki

The **TUM Wiki** provides documentation and guides for IT services:

- Service documentation and tutorials
- Step-by-step configuration guides
- Best practices and FAQs

### LRZ Documentation

The **LRZ Documentation Portal** ([doku.lrz.de](https://doku.lrz.de)) provides technical documentation for LRZ-managed services:

- Server and cloud service documentation
- Network and storage guides
- Client management resources

## IT Service Center (ITSZ)

The IT Service Center is TUM's central IT organization providing university-wide services:

- University-wide email and collaboration services
- Central identity management (TUM accounts, LDAP, Active Directory)
- Network infrastructure (MWN)
- General IT support for all TUM members
- TUMonline administration
- Domain and web services coordination

### Contact

| Channel | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Phone** | +49.89.289.17123 |
| **Email** | it-support@tum.de |
| **Website** | [www.it.tum.de](https://www.it.tum.de) |
| **Address** | Arcisstr. 21, 80333 München |

## Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ)

LRZ provides the technical infrastructure backbone for TUM and the Munich scientific community:

- High-performance computing resources
- Virtual and managed server hosting
- Online storage (NAS) and backup services
- Munich Scientific Network (MWN) operation
- Client management services (TUM-PC, MWN-MAC)
- Compute Cloud (IaaS)
- Campus software licenses and framework agreements

## Software & Hardware Procurement

TUM ensures compliant IT procurement through:

- **Framework Agreements**: LRZ-negotiated contracts with major vendors
- **Campus Licenses**: Free or reduced-price software for TUM members
- **CIP/WAP Coordination**: DFG funding programs for IT equipment (Computer Investment Program, Workstation Investment Program)

## Data Carrier Disposal

TUM provides secure disposal services for data storage media:

- Hard drives, magnetic tapes, DVDs/CDs, USB drives
- Secure destruction to protect sensitive data

::: {.callout-important}
## Data Security
Never dispose of data carriers through regular waste. Use TUM's secure disposal service to protect sensitive data.
:::
